steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Group the $x$ terms together: $6x + 5x = 11x$. The $y$ term has no like terms, so it stays as $4y$. Result: $11x + 4y$.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The simplified expression is $11x + 4y$. That matches choice C — C is correct.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '15x': Adds all three terms as if they were like terms and drops $4y$ entirely — $x$ and $y$ terms cannot be combined. ✗
  • Choice B — '15y': Treats all terms as $y$ terms and uses the wrong sum — no basis for this. ✗
  • Choice C — '11x + 4y': $6x + 5x = 11x$ and $4y$ remains unchanged. ✓
  • Choice D — '30x + 4y': Multiplies the $x$ coefficients (6 × 5 = 30) instead of adding them. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
